subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Your Internal Thermostat
description: A 60-second lesson on Your Internal Thermostat.

hook.text: Your body temperature stays at 98.6°F whether it's freezing or boiling outside. Your hypothalamus does this.

buildup.text: The hypothalamus is a tiny brain structure that constantly monitors blood temperature.

discovery.text: When you're hot, it triggers sweating and blood vessel dilation. When cold, shivering and constriction.

twist.text: 98.6°F was measured in 1851. Modern studies show normal is closer to 97.9°F—we've actually cooled.

climax.text: Understanding your thermostat helps you recognize when temperature regulation is failing.

punchline.text: A tiny brain cluster keeps you at 98°F. When it fails, emergencies begin.

quiz.question: What brain structure acts as the body's thermostat?
